title: File exists error when building container - it seems to be modifying the host not the container
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

# Description

When running my buildah script, I am getting an error that a file exists in my container when I haven't installed it yet, but it's on the host in the same location.

## Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Run: `buildah unshare ./buildah_DevOps_toolbox.sh`

## Describe the results you received:

For some reason, the commnd `install` is reporting that my applicaiton already exists when I am expecting to run this within the context of the Container, not the host. I am missing something.

<details><summary>Buildah Output</summary>

## Supporting Shell Script

<details><summary>buildah_DevOps_toolbox.sh</summary>

```bash
> cat buildah_DevOps_Toolbox.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Fail on errors
set -e

function print_output() {
  # print_output takes a string of input and formats with color the output.
  # Useful for logging.
  #
  # Parameters
  # ----------
  # $1 -> Just uses $@ to take in all input.
  #
  # Returns
  # -------
  # None
  #
  echo -e "\e[35m\e[1m==>\e[0m \e[32m\e[4m$@\e[0m"
}

function brun() {
  buildah run ${container} -- "${@}"
}

UBUNTU_VERSION=22.04
NEOVIM_BRANCH=stable

print_output "Pulling Ubuntu ${UBUNTU_VERSION}"
container=$(buildah from docker.io/library/ubuntu:${UBUNTU_VERSION})

buildah mount ${container}

print_output "Update Ubuntu"
brun sh -c "apt update && apt upgrade --yes"

brun sh -c "apt update && \
  apt install --yes wget curl apt-transport-https software-properties-common ca-certificates gnupg2 lsb-release"

print_output "Install yq"
brun sh -c "wget https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/download/v4.27.3/yq_linux_amd64 && \
  echo '9bef9dfdfdca811c307625b5d5ae75be07a382b5a7b9314d91918216965c3028  yq_linux_amd64' | sha256sum --check -- && \
  install -m 755 -d yq_linux_amd64 /usr/local/bin/yq && \
  rm yq_linux_amd64"

print_output "Install PowerShell for Linux"
# https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/install/installing-powershell-core-on-linux?view=powershell-7.1
brun sh -c"wget -q https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/${UBUNTU_VERSION}/packages-microsoft-prod.deb && \
  dpkg --install packages-microsoft-prod.deb && \
  apt update && \
  add-apt-repository --yes universe && \
  apt install --yes powershell"

print_output "Install Azure CLI"
brun sh -c "curl -sL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | \
  gpg --dearmor | \
  tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/microsoft.gpg > /dev/null && \
  echo 'deb [arg=amd64] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli/ jammy main' | \
  tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/azure-cli.list && \
  apt update && \
  apt install --yes azure-cli && \
  az extension add --name azure-devops"

print_output "Install NeoVim"
brun sh -c "add-apt-repository --yes ppa:neovim-ppa/${NEOVIM_BRANCH} && \
  apt update && \
  apt install --yes neovim python3-dev python3-pip python-is-python3 python3-neovim"

print_output "Setup NeoVim alternatives"
brun sh -c "update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/vi vi /usr/bin/nvim 60 && \
  update-alternatives --config vi && \
  update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/vim vim /usr/bin/nvim 60 && \
  update-alternatives --config vim && \
  update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/editor editor /usr/bin/nvim 60 && \
  update-alternatives --config editor"

print_output "Install kubectl from Google"
brun sh -c "apt update && \
  curl -x https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | apt-key add - && \
  echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | \
  tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list/d/kubernetes.list && \
  apt update && \
  apt install --yes kubectl"

print_output "Install Terraform"
brun sh -c "curl -fsSL https://apt.releases.hashicorp.gpg | apt-key add - && \
  apt-add-repository --yes 'deb [arch=amd64] https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com jammy main' && \
  apt update && \
  apt install --yes terraform"

print_output "Install Helm"
brun sh -c "curl https://baltocdn.com/helm/signing.asc | apt-key add - && \
  echo 'deb https://baltocdn.com/helm/stable/debian/ all main' | \
  tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/helm-stable-debian.list && \
  apt update && \
  apt install --yes helm"

print_output "Setup NodeJS"
brun sh -c "curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_16.x | bash -"

print_output "Install other libraries"
brun sh -c "apt update && \
  apt install --yes \
    git \
    tmux \
    nodejs \
    fzf \
    universal-ctags \
    sudo \
    jq
  "

print_output "Creating entrypoint script"
brun sh -c "printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n\n/usr/bin/tmux new-session nvim' | \
  tee /devops_toolbox.sh"

print_output "Create developer user and group"
brun sh -c "groupadd developer && \
  echo '%developer ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWORD:ALL' | \
  tee /etc/sudoers.d/developer && \
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/developer"

buildah config \
  --user developer \
  --workingdir /home/developer \
  --env 'TERM=xterm-256-color' \
  --env 'PATH=/home/developer/.local/bin:$PATH'
  ${container}

print_output "Install more Python applications"
brun sh -c "python -m pip install --upgrade pip && \
  python -m pip install \
    autopep8 \
    flake8 \
    pycodestyle \
    pylint \
    pytest \
    rstcheck \
    yamllint \
    jsonlint \
    ansible \
    jedi \
    pyright
  "

print_output "Install Poetry"
brun sh -c "curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 -"

print_output "Setting the Entrypoint"
buildah config \
  --entrypoint=["/usr/bin/bash"] \
  --cmd "--login -i /devops_toolbox.sh" \
  ${container}

print_output "Committing the end result container"
buildah commit --rm --squash ${container} localhost/devopstoolbox:latest

print_output "Listing available images."
buildah images
```

</details>

comments.body:
I think the issue has to do with bash passing the escaped lines to Buildah. Perhaps attempt to do the line on a single line without the \ lines.

Which exact line in your script is blowing up?

> I think the issue has to do with bash passing the escaped lines to Buildah. Perhaps attempt to do the line on a single line without the \ lines.
> 
> Which exact line in your script is blowing up?

It's blowing up at the part "Install yq" where it's attempting to

```bash
print_output "Install yq"
brun sh -c "wget https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/download/v4.27.3/yq_linux_amd64 && \
  echo '9bef9dfdfdca811c307625b5d5ae75be07a382b5a7b9314d91918216965c3028  yq_linux_amd64' | sha256sum --check -- && \
  install -m 755 -d yq_linux_amd64 /usr/local/bin/yq && \
  rm yq_linux_amd64"
```

> > install -m 755 -d yq_linux_amd64 /usr/local/bin/yq &&
> 
> Remove the `-d`

Shoot, that was supposed to be a `-D`. I'll try that out here.
That was it. I had an incorrect `install` option. I needed to use `install -D` and not `install -d`. Thank you for pointing that out to me. Stupid pinky sometimes doesn't hit those keys.
